# Recommended Labels

This file defines a practical starting label set for `agent-assist-oss`.

These labels are useful for triage, contribution clarity, and public open-source presentation.

## Core labels

| Label | Purpose | Suggested color |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `bug` | Something is broken or behaving incorrectly | `d73a4a` |
| `enhancement` | New feature or improvement idea | `a2eeef` |
| `documentation` | Docs, examples, or README improvements | `0075ca` |
| `question` | Clarification or discussion request | `d876e3` |

## Contribution labels

| Label | Purpose | Suggested color |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `good first issue` | Beginner-friendly contribution | `7057ff` |
| `help wanted` | Maintainer is inviting contributions | `008672` |

## Technical scope labels

| Label | Purpose | Suggested color |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `backend` | Backend logic, scripts, or architecture | `1d76db` |
| `api` | API behavior or endpoint work | `5319e7` |
| `tests` | Test coverage or validation work | `bfd4f2` |
| `workflow` | GitHub Actions, CI, or repo automation | `c2e0c6` |

## Suggested first setup

If you want a simple starting point, create these first:

- `bug`
- `enhancement`
- `documentation`
- `good first issue`
- `help wanted`
- `backend`
- `tests`

## Notes

The current GitHub setup already includes issue and pull request templates.

These labels complement that setup and make issues easier to organize as the project grows.
